title: 11 Herbal Creams For Nail Fungus
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great alternative for treating nail fungus. These creams often contain essential oils from various herbs that have antifungal properties. One reason herbal teas can help relieve nail fungus is because the active compounds in these herbs can penetrate deep into the nail bed, killing the fungi that cause the infection. For instance, Eucalyptus globulus contains a compound called eucalyptol, which has been shown to have antifungal properties. Similarly, Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has been used for centuries to treat fungal infections due to its potent antifungal properties. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, has also been found to have antifungal properties, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for nail fungus. Using herbal creams can be beneficial as they are often free from harsh chemicals and can be less expensive than prescription medications. They can also be a more natural and gentle approach to treating nail fungus, which can lead to fewer side effects and a lower risk of skin irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains compounds like eucalyptol, caffeic acid, and globulol that have antifungal properties. Eucalyptol helps to reduce fungal growth by disrupting the cell membrane of the fungus, making it harder for it to survive. Caffeic acid has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent the spread of fungal infections. Globulol, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the nail and surrounding skin. By using these compounds, Eucalyptus globulus creams can help to combat nail fungus and promote healthy nail growth.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water and 1 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of eucalyptus essential oil to the saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a bowl and let it cool completely before applying it to the affected nail.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains the active constituent cineole, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections. This cream also contains terpinen-4-ol, which is known for its antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungi. Additionally, the tea tree oil in Melaleuca alternifolia creams has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling associated with nail fungus. The antifungal properties of the active constituents in Melaleuca alternifolia creams help to kill the fungus that causes nail fungus, while the anti-inflammatory properties promote healing and reduce discomfort. This combination of properties makes Melaleuca alternifolia creams a potentially effective treatment for nail fungus.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 10 drops of tea tree essential oil, and 10 drops of lavender essential oil.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until melted and smooth.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and stir in distilled water. Let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add tea tree essential oil and lavender essential oil to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container. Let it cool and harden completely before use. Apply to affected nails as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two active constituents that make it effective in treating nail fungus. Thymol has antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which help to kill the fungus that causes infection. Carvacrol has antibacterial properties, which help to prevent the spread of infection and promote a healthy environment for the nail to grow. The antifungal properties of thymol also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Thymus vulgaris creams can help to eliminate the fungus that causes nail fungus, promoting healthy nail growth.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 teaspoons of Thymus vulgaris essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a saucepan over low heat, stirring until beeswax dissolves.
Add shea butter to the saucepan and stir until melted.
Remove saucepan from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in Thymus vulgaris essential oil.
Pour mixture into a container and let cool until solid. Use as a cream for nail fungus, applying 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol. These active constituents have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help combat nail fungus. Gingerol, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause nail infections. Shogaol, on the other hand, has been found to have a strong antifungal activity, making it effective in reducing fungal growth on nails. By applying Zingiber officinale creams, the active constituents can reach the affected nail area and help reduce fungal growth.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale oil (ginger oil).
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and add the Zingiber officinale oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Zingiber officinale cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor, which are its active constituents. These compounds have antifungal properties that help combat nail fungus. Linalool and linalyl acetate have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing the severity of the infection. Camphor, on the other hand, helps to dry out the affected area, creating an environment that's less conducive to fungal growth. By combining these properties, Lavandula angustifolia creams can help to alleviate symptoms of nail fungus and promote healthy nail growth.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat, then add the dried Lavandula angustifolia. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
In a separate bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax. Melt them in a double boiler or in the microwave.
Combine the cooled Lavandula angustifolia infusion with the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir until it thickens into a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams contains a high concentration of limonene, a powerful antifungal compound that helps to combat nail fungus. The creams also contain geraniol and linalool, which have antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to prevent the growth of fungi on the nails. These compounds help to create an environment that is not conducive to the growth of fungi, thus promoting healthy nail growth. By inhibiting the growth of fungi, Cymbopogon citratus creams helps to clear up yellowing or thickening of the nails, restoring their natural appearance. Regular use of Cymbopogon citratus creams can lead to a significant reduction in fungal growth and improvement in nail health.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of aloe vera gel, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of Cymbopogon citratus essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the aloe vera gel and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F. Stir in the Cymbopogon citratus essential oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely before applying to the affected nail area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains active constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have strong antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections, such as onychomycosis. Curcumin's ability to inhibit fungal growth and penetration into the nail is attributed to its high reactivity and ability to bind to fungal enzymes. The anti-inflammatory properties of Curcuma longa also help reduce swelling and redness associated with fungal infections. By targeting both the fungal infection and the inflammation, Curcuma longa creams can help promote healthy nail growth and reduce the risk of fungal infections.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, 2 tablespoons of tea tree oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Add turmeric powder and tea tree oil to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica creams contains active constituents like azadirachtin, nimbin, and nimnodal, which have antifungal properties. These compounds work by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells, specifically the dermatophyte species that cause nail fungus. Azadirachta indica also contains salannin, a compound that enhances the antifungal activity of azadirachtin. The antiseptic and antimicrobial properties of Azadirachta indica help to reduce inflammation and prevent further infection. By targeting the fungal cells and reducing inflammation, Azadirachta indica creams can help to promote healthy nail growth and treat nail fungus.
remedies.image_alt: Azadirachta indica herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Azadirachta indica oil, and 10 drops of tea tree oil.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add the Azadirachta indica oil and tea tree oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or a lip balm tube and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, your Azadirachta indica cream for nail fungus is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected nail and surrounding skin, twice a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains a compound called aloin, which has antifungal properties that help combat nail fungus. Aloin works by inhibiting the growth of fungi that cause the infection, reducing the severity of symptoms. The cream also contains anthraquinones, which help to soothe and calm the skin around the affected nail, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera in the cream help to reduce redness and swelling associated with nail fungus. Additionally, aloe vera's antimicrobial properties help to prevent the spread of the infection and promote a clean environment for the nail to heal.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 1 teaspoon of tea tree oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
In a bowl, mix the Aloe vera gel, coconut oil, and shea butter together until smooth.
Add the tea tree oil and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow it to thicken.
Apply the Aloe vera cream to the affected nail area 2-3 times a day, making sure to cover the entire nail surface.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contains a number of bioactive constituents that make it effective in treating nail fungus. The most significant of these is a compound called aloin, which has antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections. Aloe vera also contains salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort. The gel of the aloe vera plant contains glycoproteins and vitamins A, C, and E, which help to nourish and moisturize the nail, promoting healthy growth and reducing the risk of further infection. When applied topically, the antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera work together to help clear up nail fungus and promote healthy nail growth.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil. Clean and sanitize all equipment.
In a bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of tea tree oil and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil. These oils help fight nail fungus.
Add the oil mixture to the aloe vera gel and mix well. Stir until combined.
Transfer the mixture to a container and refrigerate for 30 minutes. This helps thicken the cream.
Apply the aloe vera cream to the affected nail and surrounding skin, 2-3 times a day. Cover with a bandage to keep it in place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains active constituents like bilobalide and ginkgolides, which help fight nail fungus. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and redness around the affected nail area. Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids and terpenoids also have antioxidant properties, protecting the nail from further damage and promoting a healthy environment for growth. The cream's antifungal properties, derived from ginkgolides, inhibit the growth of fungi, such as Trichophyton and Epidermophyton, that cause nail fungus. By combining these properties, Ginkgo biloba creams create an environment that helps to clear up and prevent nail fungus.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for nail fungus
remedy_procedure: Melt 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract to the mixture.
Stir well until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove from heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency.
Transfer the Ginkgo biloba cream to a container and store it in a cool, dry place.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent nail fungus from forming. Some herbal teas, like tea tree oil and peppermint, have antifungal properties that can help fight infection. These teas are believed to help keep nails healthy by promoting good circulation and reducing stress, which can contribute to nail fungus.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for nail fungus can be safe to consume daily if you choose teas from reliable sources and follow the recommended brewing instructions. Some popular options include tea tree oil, oregano, and thyme. However, be cautious of strong or bitter flavors, and adjust the amount of tea to suit your taste.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas may take a few weeks to several months to show results in nail fungus. The effectiveness depends on the tea's ingredients and the severity of the fungus. Some people notice improvement in their nails after 4-6 weeks of consistent use, while others may see results after 2-3 months.
faq_time: For nail fungus, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning. This allows the antioxidants to start fighting the fungus right away. Drinking it first thing can also help boost your immune system, which is essential for fighting off nail fungus. Morning is a great time to give your body a head start.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for nail fungus. Some herbs like garlic and ginger can thin blood, which can be a problem if you're taking medications like terbinafine that also affect blood clotting. This can increase the risk of bleeding. Other herbs like St. John's Wort can affect how your body processes certain medications, making them less effective.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially for nail fungus. Some herbal teas may not be safe for the baby, and we don't know how they'll affect the pregnancy. If you're considering trying herbal teas, look for ones that have been studied in pregnant women and follow the recommended amounts.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and considering herbal teas for nail fungus, be cautious. Some herbal teas might pass into your milk and affect your baby. For example, tea tree oil can be safe in small amounts, but excessive consumption could be a problem. It's best to check the ingredients and amounts carefully before drinking.
title_2: 11 Natural Herbal Creams for Diabetics with Nail Fungus
